notes: SHIP v1.14.273 - U-MAP 1U RATCHET (CODE-QUEUE Task 2 - the LAST open queue task). Owner: ".272 verified, lock re-armed, ship task 2". THE RACK SCENE LOCK IS RE-ARMED and this ship RESPECTS IT: Task 2 is the U-MAP COLUMN (UI), never the 3D scene. Proven in the diff: zero lines touch rackElevation_render3D / materials / lights / camera / floor / createCable / trayGroups / ortho. ==== WHAT: the U-map carriage now steps 1U at a time - totalU detents, finger continuous, carriage QUANTIZED. Positional quantization only, never rate-limiting, so a full-speed flick is unaffected and simply lands on a centre. New _umapQuant(rawPct) maps a raw finger pct to {u, pct} where u = totalU - floor(pct/100*totalU) (which U-band the finger is in) and pct = that band's CENTRE. Wired into all three carriage-movers: the minimap drag (touchstart/move + mousedown), the canvas-scroll sync path, and the first-paint. DRO + engaged band update ON the step (they take the quantized u/pct), never from the raw finger - the queue's exact requirement. Step transition: handle CSS was `top 0.1s cubic-bezier` (100ms ease-out) -> `top 0.05s linear` (<=50ms bound, mechanical click); no settle animation on release because the carriage is already on a centre by construction. Tap-to-jump unchanged (same handler, a tap now lands on a centre). ==== THE QUEUE SPECCED THIS AGAINST A MOCK: `fracToU`/`uToFrac` and "the /47 stays" describe helpers that DO NOT EXIST in this app (same class as S43's missing `buildUMap`). Implemented the INTENT against the real geometry: scrubbar_buildHtml draws U `u` as a band spanning pct [(totalU-u)/totalU, (totalU-u+1)/totalU], so "which U is the finger in" is a FLOOR over that band and the detent is the band CENTRE. ==== TWO REAL DEFECTS THIS DESIGN AVOIDS, both found by numeric proof BEFORE shipping (not by eye): [1] THE DETENT IS THE BAND CENTRE, NOT ITS TOP EDGE. engageAt's band test is inclusive at BOTH ends (>= t0 && <= t0+h0), so a top-edge detent - which sits exactly on the boundary shared by two adjacent bands - lights TWO bands on 26 of 48 U's. The centre engages exactly ONE, 48/48. [2] THE DRO NOW READS THE STEPPED u DIRECTLY, not round(totalU - pct/100*totalU). That round() is knife-edge at a band centre and float error tips it DOWN on 8 of 48 U's (U1->0, 4->3, 7->6, 10->9, 13->12, 16->15, 27->26, 30->29). Pre-quantization this was masked because the carriage rested at arbitrary positions; quantize onto centres and the OLD DRO would have named the wrong U on 8 detents while the carriage sat on the right one - a DRO/carriage disagreement, worse than no ratchet. Passing uExact into engageAt fixes it. ==== VERIFIED LIVE (real app over http, 47-slot rack, totalU 48): _umapQuant swept over 2001 finger positions 0..100% -> ALL land on a band centre; 48 distinct detents; 48 distinct Us; DRO u matches the detent on all 48; exactly ONE band engages at each of 48 (numeric, inclusive-both-ends test). Live drag via the real minimapDrag handler: 48 distinct detents hit, DRO matches carriage at every one, exactly 1 band engaged, sequence steps cleanly U48->U2. First paint lands on a centre (U42 @ 13.5417%, DRO agrees). Handle transition confirmed 0.05s linear. ?legacy=1 proven clean: no #umapDro markup (rd-gated in scrubbar_buildHtml), engageAt early-returns so 0 lt-engaged bands, scrubbar still present as always; visible DOM byte-identical (the handle position quantizing is behavior-neutral for legacy, which never had a U instrument). ==== HARNESS NOTE: rackHybrid_initSync is inside a double rAF (:33740) that never fires in a hidden automation tab, so the scroll/drag listeners aren't bound on load - had to call initSync() directly to test, same rAF-blindness seen all session. The quantizer MATH needs no rAF and was proven exhaustively. Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS 12 blocks balanced; CRLF uniform; three-stamp .272->.273. John iPhone gate: [1] slow scrub the U-map: exactly 48 clicks top to bottom, the carriage NEVER rests between U's, the DRO ticks one U per step. [2] fast flick: lands ON a centre, never between (positional quantization, not rate limit). [3] the engaged glass band flips ON the step and exactly one band lights at a time. [4] tap-to-jump still lands on a U; keyboard +/-1 and unit-select unchanged. [5] the rack SCENE is pixel-identical to .272 - the lock held (this ship never touched it). [6] ?legacy=1 the U-map behaves as before (no visible change)."
